ideation: This animation sequence simulates the rolling of a die, using both arms to mimic the tossing motion. The arms move in a coordinated manner, creating a dynamic yet controlled action. I'll draw on existing examples to ensure smooth transitions and natural-looking movements. Frame 1: Both arms are held slightly upward, palms facing each other, as if cupping the die. Frame 2: The arms are extended forward, simulating the release of the die. Frame 3: The arms swing back slightly, as if following through with the toss. Frame 4: The arms settle into a neutral position after the toss. Frame 5: The arms slowly lower back to resting position. This sequence captures the anticipation, action, and settling of the die toss. I will ensure that each frame has a clear and distinct pose, emphasizing the dynamic nature of the toss.

frames.beat: arms cup the imaginary die, palms inward
arms extend forward, releasing the die with a flick
arms follow through with a slight backward swing
arms pause mid-air after the toss
arms slowly lower to a resting position
